title: senecio pemehuensis
intro: Senecio pemehuensis, also known as the Peruvian groundsel, is a flowering plant species native to the Andean region of South America, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Peruvian groundsel has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Senecio pemehuensis can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and processing the leaves to create a tincture or capsule for later use.
